title: Reference Counting, Weak References, and Cycles · Mastery Check
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar reference counting, weak references, and cycles decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for overflow-safe reference-counted graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.
Prevent count overflow, underflow, resurrection, and release reentrancy with explicit states.
Demonstrate why pure reference counting cannot reclaim strong cycles and locate cycle-forming edges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Retain Release Invariant
steps.instruction:
Order the Retain Release Invariant evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Retain Release Invariant.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Retain Release Invariant assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Retain Release Invariant, keep Retain Release Invariant connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 4884. Produce bounded C evidence to model strong counts and expose a cycle that cannot reach zero; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Retain Release Invariant has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
